waste_signals: MCP examples used older local/package assumptions
Existing rules covered secrets but not functional hardcode
Repeated frontend attempts lacked hard loop breaker and runtime proof
Context pollution lacked explicit operational handling
Inventory drift found; no script yet
Model routing still used Cursor-only language in some paths
Token metrics were qualitative and had no report workflow
Token savings reports lacked structured percentage/retry/error fields
Ledgers were passive and not retrieved before similar missions
Legacy batch generator could recreate outdated framework artifacts
Team usage reports would require manual counting across contributors and projects
savings_signals: Remote OAuth and npx templates reduce local setup failures
Hardcode scanner and checklist reduce repeated manual review and future rework
Attempt Ledger and Boundary Map reduce repeated commands, wrong-port validation and collateral regressions
Compacted Snapshot prevents stale outputs from guiding later phases
No code scan beyond framework docs
Surface split prevents wrong model guidance between Cursor and Codex
Adds report generation with confidence level and avoids fake exact token counts
Adds baseline vs actual units for estimated savings reports
Execution Memory, Evidence Anchoring and Post-Mission Evaluation reduce repeated reads, unsupported conclusions and repeated mistakes
Removing generator prevents accidental large stale rewrites; incremental skill template remains
Collector consolidates existing ledgers into local, project and team reports without exposing prompts or secrets

data_quality.measurement_type: estimated operational units, not real tokens
data_quality.privacy: No prompts, secrets, long code excerpts or private data
